427.reason: [Operator precedence issue: `||` has lower precedence than `&&`, causing the condition to be parsed as `curType == CONSTTK || (isBType && ...)`, which may incorrectly treat a function definition as a declaration when `curType` is `CONSTTK` and the next token is not an identifier.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Operator precedence issue: `||` has lower precedence than `&&`, causing the condition to be parsed as `curType == CONSTTK || (isBType && ...)`, which may incorrectly treat a function definition as a declaration when `curType` is `CONSTTK` and the next token is not an identifier.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Operator precedence issue: `||` has lower precedence than `&&`, causing the condition to be evaluated as `curType == LexType.CONSTTK || ((isBType(curType)) && getNextType() == LexType.IDENFR && getNext2Type() != LexType.LPARENT)`, which may incorrectly treat a function definition as a declaration when `curType` is `CONSTTK` but the next tokens indicate a function.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 1.0 , reason:  // If no words were produced by the lexer (e.g., empty input), curType is null, causing NullPointerException here.

430.reason: [Logic error: The condition `getNextType() != LexType.MAINTK` is always true when `curType` is a function type, because `MAINTK` is a separate token for "main", not a function type. This loop will never execute for `main` function definitions, but it may incorrectly skip other function definitions if the next token is `MAINTK` (which shouldn't happen). The intended logic should check if the next token is `IDENFR` (function name) rather than `MAINTK`.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 0.5 , reason:  [Logic error: The condition `getNextType() != LexType.MAINTK` is always true when `curType` is a function type, because `MAINTK` is a separate token for "main", not a function type. This loop will never execute for `main` function definitions, but it may incorrectly skip other function definitions if the next token is `MAINTK` (which shouldn't happen). The intended logic should check if the next token is `IDENFR` (function name) rather than `MAINTK`.]

425.reason: NullPointerException: curWord is null when next() is called first time from run() because curWord is never initialized. Also, after reading all words, curPos++ may exceed size, and curWord remains null, causing subsequent calls to fail.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  NullPointerException: curWord is null when next() is called first time from run() because curWord is never initialized. Also, after reading all words, curPos++ may exceed size, and curWord remains null, causing subsequent calls to fail.

335.reason: The arrow syntax for switch expressions is only supported in Java 14+, causing a compilation error in older Java environments.

reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The arrow syntax for switch expressions is only supported in Java 14+, causing a compilation error in older Java environments.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  The enhanced switch syntax using arrow labels (->) was introduced in Java 14. Using it in older Java environments (e.g., Java 8 or 11) causes a compilation error.

1121.reason: [The error line number should correspond to the line of the last non-terminal symbol before the expected token, not the last consumed token. Using lastWord.getLineNum() may point to an incorrect line number for error reporting as per problem requirements.]
